_comment: Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). S700123 is an HBP 1.0 code.
procedure_name: Internal Urethrotomy including cystoscopy as an independent procedure
clinical_indications: Functional disorders of bladder and Urethra

preauth.label: Clinical Notes detailing the signs and symptoms and need for surgery
Retrograde Urethrogram/ Micturating Cystourethrography reports confirming the diagnosis

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes including history, symptoms, signs, vitals, examination findings, planned line of treatment and advice for admission?
Was the Retrograde Urethrogram/ Micturating Cystourethrography report submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Indoor case papers submitted?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes submitted?
Was the Endoscopic Intra procedure still photograph submitted?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical notes and/or Retrograde Urethrogram/Micturating Cystourethrography not indicative of the necessity of the procedure.
Clinical notes detailing signs and symptoms, and need for surgery are missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
Retrograde Urethrogram/Micturating Cystourethrography reports are missing or do not confirm the diagnosis at pre-authorization.
The endoscopic intra-procedure still photograph is missing or unclear at claim submission.
Detailed Procedure/Operation notes are missing or do not adequately describe the surgical steps and findings at claim submission.
Discharge summary is missing or lacks follow-up advice at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Internal urethrotomy, is a surgical procedure used to treat urethral stricture disease, involves using a urethrotomy or a surgical knife passed through a cystoscope to widen a narrowed urethra.
Urethrotomies are generally only performed on men, as urethral strictures in women are very, very rare.
surgical_options: Minimally invasive method - Direct Visual Internal Urethrotomy (DVIU)
